title: Decide repo/branch exposed to the judge's designated GitHub account for tech-prize evaluation
source: Official 'AI Engine x Syphonix MoMQ Hack - Technology Prize' pack received 2026-06-21 (Downloads/AI Engine x Syphonix MoMQ Hack - Technology Prize.docx); summarized in memory reference_tech_prize_criteria.
why: The pack states judges will ask us to ADD A DESIGNATED GITHUB ACCOUNT to the project to evaluate it. IP stays ours and code is not shared with general contestants, but the judge gets read access to the WHOLE repo - including the barbell thesis, lottery sleeve, CEO-reserved decisions, and operations runbook. We must consciously decide what we expose (full repo vs a curated branch/mirror) before granting access.
value_rationale: M: low effort but load-bearing for confidentiality of strategy posture; getting it wrong leaks the full competitive thesis to a judge unnecessarily.
scope: Decide: grant access to the full repo, or prepare a curated branch/mirror for the judge.
Identify anything strategy-sensitive that should not be in the evaluated tree (CEO call).
Confirm the mechanics of adding the designated GitHub account when the invite arrives (post-24 Jun).
recommendation: Default to exposing the full repo (the architecture IS the pitch) unless the CEO flags specific strategy artifacts to curate out. Confirm with CEO before granting.
out_of_scope: Does not change any code; an operational/confidentiality decision.

history.reason: Surfaced from the official Technology Prize pack review (2026-06-21).
